title: Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise for now || Aframax Med exodus
section_path: Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise for now || Aframax Med exodus
section_path_text: Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise for now || Aframax Med exodus
summary:
Main
This week we put the spotlight on Red Sea transits both north- and southbound: Russian crude heading southbound and middle distillates heading northbound. In the West, we examine the effect of Libyan oil field strikes on Aframaxes, and we discuss Atlantic Basin MR oversupply. By Mary Melton

ANALYSIS / EAST OF SUEZ / DIRTY


Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise, but recent attacks may prompt diversions
Image reference: unnamed - 2024-01-18T141517.865.png | assets/2024-01-18_russian-crude-red-sea-transits-stabilise-for-now-aframax-med-exodus_img_unnamed-2024-01-18t141517865_b0be79c42417.png
Daily number of Southbound Bab el Mandeb transits made by tankers carrying Russian and non-Russian crude (7-day moving average)
Crude volumes heading Southbound via Bab-el-Mandeb have declined by almost 50% since late November 2023.

children.title: Main
children.summary: This week we put the spotlight on Red Sea transits both north- and southbound: Russian crude heading southbound and middle distillates heading northbound. In the West, we examine the effect of Libyan oil field strikes on Aframaxes, and we discuss Atlantic Basin MR oversupply.

children.title: Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise, but recent attacks may prompt diversions
section_path: Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise, but recent attacks may prompt diversions
children.section_path_text: Russian crude Red Sea transits stabilise, but recent attacks may prompt diversions
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2024-01-18T141517.865.png | assets/2024-01-18_russian-crude-red-sea-transits-stabilise-for-now-aframax-med-exodus_img_unnamed-2024-01-18t141517865_b0be79c42417.png
Daily number of Southbound Bab el Mandeb transits made by tankers carrying Russian and non-Russian crude (7-day moving average)
Crude volumes heading Southbound via Bab-el-Mandeb have declined by almost 50% since late November 2023. However, Russian crude volumes transiting the area as well as the number of transits have stabilised in the past few weeks.

children.title: Aframaxes ballast from East Med to USG as Sharara field remains closed
section_path: Aframaxes ballast from East Med to USG as Sharara field remains closed
children.section_path_text: Aframaxes ballast from East Med to USG as Sharara field remains closed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2024-01-18T141741.369.png | assets/2024-01-18_russian-crude-red-sea-transits-stabilise-for-now-aframax-med-exodus_img_unnamed-2024-01-18t141741369_234ea7a909c7.png
Number of Aframaxes ballasting from Europe to USG (LHS) vs TD19 freight rates (RHS)
The El Sharara oil field (300kbd production capacity) in Libya is currently closed due to force majeure imposed by Libya's NOC. As a result of this, crude exports from Libya's Zawia Terminal Port - which is fed by this field via pipeline - have declined by 120kbd in January (days 1-15) m-o-m.
